name: Ruta de los Sentidos (GR 85): Vuelta al Cerro de las Cruces
name_original: Del Arroyo de Gándara al Río Nela — circular desde San Cibrián
description: Hike a moderate 6.9-mile circular route from San Cibrián, exploring the Gándara Stream, Nela River, Las Pisas Waterfall, and Quintanabaldo.
region_intro: Hiking around Valle De Valdebezana offers diverse landscapes, characterized by verdant valleys, mountainous terrain, and extensive forests. The region features the Montes del Somo and El Escudo, defining its northern border, alongside the expansive Ebro Reservoir. Dense woodlands of beech and oak, river valleys shaped by the Trifón and Gándara rivers, and dramatic gorges like Desfiladero de Las Palancas provide varied terrain for exploration. Best hiking trails around Valle De Valdebezana The most popular hiking route is Ruta de los Sentidos (GR 85): Vuelta al Cerro de las Cruces, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete, offering an in-depth experience of varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Visit to the Pyramids of the Italians - circular from Corconte, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route explores the area around Corconte, near the Ebro Reservoir, with gentle relief and scenic views. Local hikers also love the Las Pisas Waterfall loop from Cascada del Molino, a 2.0 miles (3.2 km) trail leading through the magical beech forest of Las Pisas, often completed in about 55 minutes. Hiking around Valle De Valdebezana is defined by the expansive Ebro Reservoir, dense forests of beech and oak, and dramatic river gorges. The network offers options for easy walks to more moderate trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Valle De Valdebezana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Valle De Valdebezana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: Valle De Valdebezana offers a good selection of routes, with over 10 hiking trails to explore. These range from easy walks to more moderate paths, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Valle De Valdebezana has several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Pisas Waterfall loop from Cascada del Molino&lt;/a&gt; is a short, easy walk through a beautiful beech forest, often completed in under an hour. The areas around the Ebro Reservoir also offer routes with gentle relief.

Q: What kind of landscapes and terrain can I expect on hikes in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: The region boasts diverse landscapes, from verdant valleys and mountainous terrain to extensive forests of beech and oak. You'll find picturesque settings around the Ebro Reservoir, dramatic river gorges like the Desfiladero de Las Palancas, and open, solitary páramos. A significant portion of the municipality is also integrated into the Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Natural Park, offering protected natural spaces.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges to visit?
A: Absolutely. The magical beech forest of Las Pisas leads to the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4979424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Pisas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is particularly impressive during rainy seasons and snowmelt. You can also explore the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Las Palancas&lt;/a&gt;, a gorge formed by the Serna stream. For more options, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2581741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turquoise Pools and Waterfalls of Orbaneja del Castillo&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: Yes, circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047216835/visit-to-the-pyramids-of-the-italians-circular-from-corconte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit to the Pyramids of the Italians - circular from Corconte&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the area around the Ebro Reservoir. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Palancas Gorge loop from Barrio de la Mota&lt;/a&gt;, offering a scenic exploration of the gorge.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil forests and the expansive Ebro Reservoir to the dramatic gorges and waterfalls. The variety of trails, from easy walks to more challenging routes, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region blends nature with history. You can find rupestrian hermitages near Arnedo, such as La Tía Isidora and El Horno, which offer a unique historical interest. The Romanesque legacy, including the Church of San Vicente de Villamezán, and the 15th-century Castillo de los Porras in Virtus, also add cultural charm to the landscape.

Q: Is there a long-distance hiking trail that passes through Valle De Valdebezana?
A: Yes, the GR-85, known as the "Ruta de los Sentidos" (Route of the Senses), traverses the region. This long-distance path offers an in-depth experience of the varied landscapes and cultural heritage of the Las Merindades region, passing through key natural attractions like the Hayedo y Salto de las Pisas and the Desfiladero de Las Palancas.

Q: What natural features are prominent around the Ebro Reservoir?
A: The Ebro Reservoir, often called an "interior freshwater sea," is a significant natural feature. It's an ornithological paradise and provides a picturesque setting for hiking along its shores. The surrounding relief is generally gentle, offering a contrast to the more rugged mountainous areas of the valley.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: The region offers beauty year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly pleasant for hiking due to milder temperatures and vibrant foliage. Waterfalls like Salto de las Pisas are most impressive during rainy seasons and snowmelt, typically in late winter or early spring. Summer can be warm, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are there any specific natural parks or protected areas to explore?
A: Yes, a significant part of the municipality, specifically the Valle de Arreba, is integrated into the Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Natural Park. This area is part of the Natura 2000 network, designated for its ecological value, providing protected natural spaces for hikers to explore its unique flora and fauna.
tips: Quintanabaldo is a small town located at the foot of the Tejuela mountain, which offers a wall right next to the town itself. The population is crossed by the Río Nela.
This is a small stream that passes through the town of San Cibrián and where to the west there is a small waterfall that is not far from the mentioned town.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts in San Cibrián, right next to a dedicated parking lot. This makes it easy to begin your hike without worrying about finding a spot.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This is a moderate hike, suitable for most people with a good level of physical fitness. The paths are generally accessible, making it a substantial but manageable outdoor experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the circular route?
faqs.answer: Hikers can expect to complete this circular route in approximately 3 hours and 16 minutes, covering about 11.1 kilometers.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of natural beauty and local charm. You'll encounter the beautiful Las Pisas Waterfall and pass through the small town of Quintanabaldo, situated at the foot of Tejuela mountain, where the Nela River flows.

faqs.question: Is the From the Gándara Stream to the Nela River — circular from San Cibrián trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas and a small town, there's no specific information provided regarding dog restrictions. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through settlements or near wildlife, and to always clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to hike this specific trail. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal details aren't provided, trails featuring waterfalls and rivers are often most vibrant in spring with snowmelt or after rainfall, and pleasant in early summer or autumn when temperatures are milder. Consider checking local weather before your visit.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: The trail features generally easily accessible paths. You'll experience a mix of natural terrain as you follow the Gándara Stream and Nela River, and pass through the small town of Quintanabaldo.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ruta de los Sentidos, the Camino Olvidado (Villasante-Bimón), and the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in San Cibrián?
faqs.answer: The provided information focuses on the trail itself and mentions passing through a small town. For specific details on cafes, pubs, or accommodation in San Cibrián or the broader Valle de Mena area, it's best to check local listings or travel guides.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and 11.1 km length, the trail is best suited for families with older children who are accustomed to hiking. Younger children might find the distance and elevation gain challenging, though the accessible paths are a plus.
